input.background: We select an SRS of n=712 from a population of 20-29-year-old men and calculate x̄=183 pounds. The population standard deviation (σ) is 40. We propose to calculate 90%, 95%, and 99% confidence intervals for μ based on this information.
input.question: Calculate the 90% confidence interval for μ.

meta_info.caption: Confidence intervals, various levels of confidence (Body weight, 20-29-year-old U.S. males)
meta_info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\mu$ When $\sigma$ Is Known
